job_title: Revenue Technology - Data Strategy & Operations Lead

fields.question: Will you now or at any time in the future require visa sponsorship?*

fields.answer: As the first Business Operations & Strategy hire, you're looking for a generalist who can own pricing, strategic planning, and financial modeling while standing up new functions from scratch — which is exactly the work I've been doing. At M Science I led a cross-functional overhaul of our pricing and packaging model and stood up a pricing desk that drove a ~35% ARR increase in its first quarter, then built the financial and data infrastructure to make it repeatable. I'm drawn to Hang because it lets me do that end-to-end, working directly with the CEO on a consumer-brand platform I'd genuinely enjoy helping build.

fields.answer: Closest to product operations: I own the unit-economics model for M Science's KPI API product line, weighing operational and tooling spend against ARR, and I partner with sales and marketing leadership on go-to-market spend decisions. I have not formally owned a headcount or marketing OpEx budget line in a dedicated FP&A capacity.

fields.answer: I built an automated data-QA layer for our KPI API product using an OCR/LLM pipeline plus ML anomaly detection, which cut the product's annual data error rate from over 2% to near zero.

Additional Information
fields.answer: I'm applying because Product Operations is the work I gravitate toward: owning the cross-functional problems that don't have a clean owner, then building the systems that make execution repeatable. At M Science I led a ground-up redesign of our packaging and pricing model, aligning data science, sales, and research and standing up a pricing desk, which drove a ~35% ARR increase the quarter after launch. I then built the infrastructure to make it stick, conceptualizing a Salesforce master-data system that unified our product metadata and automated customer entitlements. I do my own analysis in SQL and Python rather than waiting on a queue. OpenAI is shipping capability faster than most organizations can operationalize, and closing the gap between what the product can do and how a company actually runs on it is where I do my best work.

fields.question: I acknowledge that I have opened, read, and understood the Arbitration Agreement. I understand that by submitting my application, I am agreeing to be bound by the terms of the Arbitration Agreement.
I confirm I have read the above.
Additional Information
fields.answer: I've spent my career as a strategy and operations operator, running the analytical and system-level work behind revenue growth. At M Science I led a cross-functional overhaul of our pricing and packaging model, aligning sales, data science, and research and standing up a pricing desk, which drove a ~35% ARR increase the quarter after launch. I then built the infrastructure to make it durable: a Salesforce master data system for product metadata and entitlements, plus north-star metrics, Databricks pipelines, and executive dashboards. A central cadence role owning the forecasting and operating rhythm across GTM is exactly the kind of high-leverage, ambiguous problem I want to work on, and I'm self-sufficient in SQL and Python to do the analysis myself.

fields.question: I acknowledge that I have opened, read, and understood the Arbitration Agreement. I understand that by submitting my application, I am agreeing to be bound by the terms of the Arbitration Agreement.
I confirm I have read the above.
Additional Information
fields.answer: I build data-intensive products for financially sophisticated users, so this role sits right where I want to be. At M Science I own our KPI API, which forecasts critical financial metrics for hundreds of companies for institutional and quant investors. Through discovery I found the API's restatement structure was creating look-ahead bias that broke clients' models, then partnered with engineering on a point-in-time schema that fixed it, doubled the number of quant teams using the data, and grew product ARR by about 80%. I also built ML anomaly detection and LLM-based QA that cut our error rate from over 2% to near zero. Turning frontier AI into financial tooling that teams trust enough to make real decisions with is exactly the kind of problem I want to work on.

fields.question: I acknowledge that I have opened, read, and understood the Arbitration Agreement. I understand that by submitting my application, I am agreeing to be bound by the terms of the Arbitration Agreement.
I confirm I have read the above.
Will you now or at any time in the future require visa sponsorship?*
From where do you intend to work?*
Do you have at least 7+ years of direct product management experience?*
Do you have at least 2+ years in lending, credit, payments, or another regulated financial domain?*
If you answered 'Yes' to the question above, then please describe your experience. *
fields.answer: My most directly relevant experience is at PennyMac Loan Services, where I spent about a year and a half as a Data Analyst in mortgage lending. I was the Broker Direct data domain expert and senior point of contact, built the high-visibility SQL and Excel reporting leadership used to steer strategy across a large loan portfolio, and produced time-sensitive regulatory reports, so I've worked inside the compliance and data rigor a regulated lending business demands. Since then, at M Science I've spent three-plus years as a Product Manager building data products for institutional and quant investors, which keeps me fluent in financial-services data and the needs of a highly regulated customer base. That combination of hands-on mortgage-lending data experience and product ownership in financial services is why Mercury's business lending mandate resonates with me: I understand both how much the lending lifecycle depends on trustworthy data and decisioning, and how to turn that into products customers trust.

fields.question: Additional Information
fields.answer: I've spent my career shipping data-intensive products and building things from zero. At M Science I own our KPI API, where product discovery surfaced adoption blockers the quant teams hadn't framed as product problems; the schema we rebuilt with engineering nearly doubled the teams using the data and grew ARR by ~80%. I'm also a builder by instinct. At Audare AI I used prototyping tools like Bolt and Lovable to get concepts in front of users early instead of arguing them in a doc. ChatGPT Sites sits right at the intersection I care about: putting real AI capability into a consumer product people actually use to make something. That's the kind of problem I want to work on, and I'd bring hands-on discovery, a bias for building, and comfort operating at the lowest level of detail.
